acceptance_criteria: openpyxl successfully parses ore_reconcile_2.xlsx with no parse errors
Formulas for variance and metal impact are present as cell formulas and reference input cells
absolute_variance_flag and geology_review_flag columns exist and are populated for baseline and O1/O2/O3
expert_memo.md contains verification snippet and canonical final_score = 0.75 (or documented justification if changed)
A small parse/validation log (openpyxl_parse_log.txt) is included
extra_model_fields.notes_from_verifier: Also ensure the final artifacts clearly label which numeric score is canonical so graders/verifiers do not have conflicting top-level score values.

acceptance_criteria: openpyxl successfully parses ore_reconcile_2.xlsx with no parse errors

planned_changes: Regenerate ore_reconcile_2.xlsx using a standard .xlsx writer (prefer openpyxl/xlsxwriter). If those libraries are unavailable in the worker environment, produce OOXML but validate the file with openpyxl locally and include a validation log in the attempt.

Implement formulas in cells for: grade variance = ABS(plant_grade_pct - model_grade_pct) and metal impact = tonnes * (plant_grade_pct - model_grade_pct). Do not hard-code final numeric results only; ensure formulas reference the input cells or a scenario input sheet.

Add explicit columns: absolute_variance_flag (boolean or 0/1) with formula =IF(ABS(plant-model)>12,TRUE,FALSE) and geology_review_flag set accordingly. Populate these for baseline and for each remediation scenario (O1/O2/O3) in separate columns or sheets.

Update expert_memo.md to include: (a) a verification snippet showing cell addresses and formula text for one baseline row and one scenario; (b) an explicit canonical final_score statement and brief note explaining the deterministic artifact-contract precheck score if retained; (c) brief notes on workbook generation method and a small dump/screenshot of formulas if manual OOXML used.
Run a local check that openpyxl can parse the new workbook and add the parsing success/log to the artifacts (e.g., openpyxl_parse_log.txt).
Resubmit 245-revised with the updated workbook and memo, and include a short README that lists the acceptance checks and how a reviewer can reproduce the calculations.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve reproducibility and traceability: make spreadsheet formulas verifiable, add explicit per-row flags for baseline and scenarios, and reconcile score reporting.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts are present and calculations in the memo are correct, but reproducibility and traceability are weak: the spreadsheet's in-cell formulas could not be programmatically verified (openpyxl unavailable) and the memo/spreadsheet do not show explicit per-row geology_review/absolute_variance_flag columns for baseline and for each remediation scenario. Also, grader outputs contain inconsistent score fields (deterministic artifact score 1.0 vs model final_score 0.75); clarify canonical score in the revision.
